items.area: Stakeholder / contact-role coverage on the opportunity

sources: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio)
metrics.narrative: Evan Bartholio described qualitative pain on the 4 May 2026 call: reliance on ERP-derived historical data for supplier risk with no visibility into geopolitical or financial risk factors, and a stated fatigue with manual reporting ('tired of just looking at reports and looking at data... let's have some automations and see if AI can do any of the heavy lifting'). No cost, volume, or ROI figures have been quantified by either buyer contact.

sources: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio)
champion.narrative: Evan Bartholio is the primary contact and shows genuine curiosity, self-identifying as someone 'leading some of the AI initiatives within the supply chain and operations' at Rocket Lab, and he proactively named the three modules to prioritize. However, there is no evidence of internal advocacy, access to an economic buyer, or any action taken by him since the 4 May 2026 call: the discovery call he agreed to schedule has not materialized, and a rep re-approach on 22 Jun 2026 has gone unanswered. He is best read as an interested exploratory contact, not a confirmed champion.

sources: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio, Israel Powell)
competition.narrative: No named competing vendor has surfaced. The real alternative is inertia: Rocket Lab runs its MRP/ERP on Infor, uses a separate system for RFx/quoting and supplier communications, and does supplier-risk analysis manually off ERP data. With no urgency signal or executive push recorded, continuing on the current patchwork (do-nothing) is the live threat, not a rival vendor.

sources: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is real but narrow in evidence: Evan Bartholio confirmed manual, ERP-dependent processes for supplier risk ('beyond historical data and current data, we don't really have a good pulse on other factors'), no dedicated CLM ('would have to loop in someone from legal'), and reliance on Infor plus a separate quoting tool for sourcing events. He also confirmed Govcloud/government-contract restrictions apply to the relevant stakeholder environment, an unresolved constraint on any solution.

paper_process.narrative: Commercial and contracting mechanics have not been discussed. The deal is pre-discovery-call at Qualified stage; no pricing, procurement, or legal conversation has occurred.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or executive sponsor has been named or engaged. The only two contacts on record are Evan Bartholio (Business Systems Manager) and Israel Powell (Manager, Supply Chain Operations), both mid-level with no stated budget authority. Neither call surfaced a name for who controls procurement spend at Rocket Lab.

decision_process.narrative: No process has been defined. Evan Bartholio stated he first needs to 'gather the actual pain points, talk to the people who are living them instead of just myself' before a discovery call, and that discovery call has not been booked in the ~9 weeks since. No approval chain, committee, or timeline has been described.

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria exist yet. Evan Bartholio named three areas of interest (sourcing/RFx, supplier risk & performance management, Merlin Intake with the relevant stakeholder integration) on 4 May 2026, but described Rocket Lab as being at an 'exploration phase, like, just seeing kind of what capabilities are out there' with no documented requirements or scoring criteria.

forecast_read.math: 77 days to close (24 Sep 2026 vs 9 Jul 2026 today); ~70 days since creation (30 Apr 2026); ~65 days since last verified activity (5 May 2026); ~66 days since the sole substantive call (4 May 2026); the standard Zycus enterprise motion runs 12-15 months, so a 24 Sep 2026 close from a pre-discovery-call Qualified stage is not credible on the standard motion.
forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the honest read: the deal is at Qualified with one exploratory buyer call, no economic buyer, and no discovery call held yet. No evidence supports anything more conviction-bearing.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling: 65 days have passed since the last verified buyer activity (5 May 2026), and a rep re-approach on 22 Jun 2026 has not produced a reply. Both Avoma touchpoints available were fully read, but there is genuinely only one substantive buyer conversation to draw on for this deal.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-04-30, qualified same day, 69 days in Qualified stage with zero progression signals. Close date 2026-09-24 (78 days out) requires 10-11x the normal enterprise velocity from this stage. LastActivityDate 2026-05-05 (64 days ago); rep reached out 2026-06-22 with no buyer reply. Standard motion from Qualified to close is 12-15 months (discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, shortlist, workshops, commercials, negotiation, contracting); this deal has 78 days budgeted and has not yet held a discovery call. No submission-date milestones recorded. Forecast is Pipeline, which is appropriate, but the close date is fantasy.

69 days in Qualified stage with no stage progression

north_star_verdict.headline: Cooling pre-discovery deal with no buyer engagement in 64 days, no power sponsors, and an indefensible 78-day close date from Qualified stage.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Evan Bartholio: only 2 contact roles are logged and no one but Evan has engaged since 4 May 2026; bring in Israel Powell and other supply-chain/IT candidates before the discovery call.
Close the loop on the discovery call Evan proposed for the week of 8-15 May 2026 before the exploration window cools further; over 9 weeks have passed with no confirmed booking.
best_practice_check.summary: The two highest-leverage next moves are re-establishing buyer contact and multi-threading; the deal has no credibility-vs-competition issue since there is no active rival, only inertia.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus over any alternative has been recorded; the single call on 4 May 2026 was an exploratory platform overview, and the buyer described Rocket Lab as being at an 'exploration phase.'

competitive_position.summary: The only competitive force in evidence is inertia: Rocket Lab currently runs procurement on Infor (MRP/ERP) plus manual supplier-risk tracking and a separate RFx/quoting tool. No named rival vendor has surfaced in any call or field.

competitors.name: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
competitors.quote: we do our best with using that data to either, like, look at trends, do some advanced analysis, and maybe forecast some risk... but beyond that, I think that's where a potential gap is or a need for us
competitors.source: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as consolidating Rocket Lab's point solutions (Infor MRP/ERP, a separate RFx/quoting tool, and manual supplier-risk tracking) into one platform, reducing total cost of ownership while adding the AI-driven automation Rocket Lab currently lacks.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early, single-threaded, cooling deal: one substantive buyer conversation on 4 May 2026 generated real interest across three modules, but no economic buyer is engaged, the discovery call promised afterward has never been booked, and a 22 Jun 2026 rep re-approach has gone unanswered — consistent with a low Win read and a stalling Momentum read for a Qualified-stage opportunity.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded: no stakeholder beyond the two named contacts has engaged since 4 May 2026, and the opportunity carries only 2 contact roles in Salesforce, below the multi-thread bar.

An outstanding Zycus commitment — confirming Govcloud/government-contract functionality limitations, raised 4 May 2026 — remains unanswered after roughly 9 weeks, a genuine blocker for a government-contractor account.
Close-date credibility: a 24 Sep 2026 close (77 days away) is not supportable from a Qualified, pre-discovery-call deal on Zycus's typical 12-15 month enterprise motion.

win_position.text: No economic buyer or executive sponsor is engaged; only a Business Systems Manager and a Supply Chain Operations Manager have participated, capping win confidence at this early Qualified stage (contact roles; call, 4 May 2026).

The single deep conversation held, on 4 May 2026, covered Sourcing, Supplier Risk/AI monitoring, and Merlin Intake/Teams integration in real depth, with the buyer naming those three as the areas most relevant to Rocket Lab's manual, Infor-centric procurement process.

The only alternative in evidence is inertia: Rocket Lab currently runs procurement on Infor plus manual tracking and a separate quoting tool, and with no urgency signal or sponsor, doing nothing is a live risk (call, 4 May 2026).

The close date of 24 Sep 2026 (77 days out) implies roughly a 5-month total cycle for a multi-module enterprise deal that has not yet held its discovery call, well short of the standard 12-15 month enterprise motion.

deal_momentum.text: 65 days have passed since the last verified buyer engagement (activity logged 5 May 2026) with no two-way buyer contact since.
A rep re-approach logged 22 Jun 2026 (~2.5 weeks ago) has produced no buyer reply as of this sweep — a rep-side send into silence, not momentum.

The single buyer call held (4 May 2026) was genuinely substantive: two buyer-side attendees engaged for over 35 minutes on product depth, and the buyer proactively named next-step scope (materials on 3 modules, then a discovery call).
The discovery call the buyer agreed to schedule 'late this week or next' after 4 May 2026 has never been booked, meaning the deal has not advanced past its first conversation in over 9 weeks.

customer_commitment.text: Only two contacts are engaged (a Business Systems Manager and a Supply Chain Operations Manager), both mid-level, with no evidence of an economic buyer, senior sponsor, or additional department stakeholders as of 4 May 2026.

The primary buyer contact proactively named specific modules of interest (Sourcing, Supplier Risk & Performance Management, Merlin Intake/Teams) and committed to gathering pain points from colleagues before the next call (4 May 2026).

hard.ais_why:
• Evan Bartholio joined as the Business Systems Engineer driving operations, supply chain, and AI initiatives. An explicit AI mandate from the prospect's own seat.
• Direct customer voice: the team is tired of just looking at reports and wants automation and AI to do the heavy lifting.
• Merlin Intake, autonomous negotiation, and supplier scorecards are all named areas of interest. Next steps scope Merlin Intake, supplier risk, and Teams integration follow-up.

value.quote: we do our best with using that data to either, like, look at trends, do some advanced analysis, and maybe forecast some risk... but beyond that, I think that's where a potential gap is or a need for us
value.source: Avoma call 'Introduction to Zycus', 4 May 2026 (Evan Bartholio)
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as consolidating Rocket Lab's point solutions (Infor MRP/ERP, a separate RFx/quoting tool, and manual supplier-risk tracking) into one platform, reducing total cost of ownership while adding the AI-driven automation Rocket Lab currently lacks.

value.name: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
value.quote: We've been doing a lot of reporting on our own with data, and we've gotten good at that. But it's like, alright, tired of just looking at reports and looking at data, let's have some automations.
value.how_we_win: Use the Merlin Intake / Ana agentic story to show a concrete before/after on the manual reporting and supplier-risk gap Evan already named, rather than competing against another vendor.

packets.subject: Status quo (Infor ERP + manual/third-party tools, do-nothing)
